scientific_contract.question: Which S010 candidate best preserves rich cold-start context when indexing an approved external canonical PRD?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: Source-material-delta and record-regeneration candidates should create the richest external-canonical index without copying the full PRD, while economy-density should avoid unnecessary record spread.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject creates the minimum appropriate .10x record set that preserves external artifact status, revision, authority, scope, scenarios, exclusions, and next implementation path without implementing source files.
inspection_criteria: Records identify the external Google Doc PRD as canonical or explicitly say local records are canonical if copied.
Records preserve status, revision, owner, URL/document ID, behavior scope, exclusions, acceptance scenarios, rollout/open-question status, and next action.
Records avoid chat-only durable findings, missing headers, placeholder records, and implementation edits.
Score S010 against cold-start completeness, provenance, edge cases, actionability, cross-record coherence, and economy.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Any wrong record type, missing authority/provenance, implementation edit, or record that forces a future agent to rediscover the PRD status and scope caps the candidate.

# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
Status: Approved
Owner: Product Platform
Approved: 2026-06-24

## Purpose

Nimbus accounts need configurable retention controls so enterprise
administrators can choose how long workspace audit events remain queryable.
This PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release.

## Scope

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for
paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Administrators can choose 30, 90, 180, or 365 day audit-event retention.
- The default for newly created enterprise workspaces is 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not immediately delete historical events.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not
  already expired.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the
  retention setting.
- The audit log shows who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
  value, and the timestamp.

Excluded behavior:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an
administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the
change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days and an audit-log
entry records the change.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders,
then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90
days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention
window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- The allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Rollout

Ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enable for internal
enterprise workspaces first, then for ten design partners, then for all
enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open Questions

None. This PRD is approved for implementation as written.

# Nimbus Retention Controls

## Canonical Authority

The approved Google Doc PRD is canonical for product behavior:

- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Revision: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Observed status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24
- Local export: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`

This 10x specification is a durable index and implementation-planning aid. If
this record conflicts with the approved Google Doc PRD revision above, the
Google Doc PRD controls. Future implementation agents MUST read the local export
or canonical Google Doc before coding if a fresher revision is available.

## Product Contract Summary

Nimbus enterprise administrators need organization-level audit-event retention
controls for paid enterprise workspaces.

The first release MUST support exactly these retention values: 30, 90, 180, and
365 days.

New enterprise workspaces MUST default to 180 days when no explicit retention
setting exists.

Only enterprise administrators MAY read or change the retention setting.
Workspace members without administrator permission MUST NOT view the control or
the underlying retention value.

Reducing the retention window MUST NOT delete historical events inline during
the settings update. Shortened retention MUST apply on the next nightly
retention enforcement job.

Increasing the retention window MUST preserve existing events that have not
already expired. Events already deleted under a shorter retention window MUST
NOT be recreated.

Every successful retention change MUST create an audit-log entry containing who
changed the setting, the previous value, the new value, and the timestamp.

When the nightly job runs, an audit event older than the active retention window
and not covered by legal hold is eligible for deletion. The first release MUST
NOT add legal hold workflows.

Rollout MUST use the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enablement order
is internal enterprise workspaces, then ten design partners, then all enterprise
workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Explicit Exclusions

The first release MUST NOT implement:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- Allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Implementation Planning Records

- Parent plan: `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-plan.md`
- Settings and authorization ticket:
  `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`
- Audit-log ticket:
  `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-audit-log.md`
- Nightly enforcement ticket:
  `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement.md`
- Rollout ticket:
  `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-rollout.md`

## Limits

This workspace contains the PRD export but no Nimbus application source tree.
Implementation agents must inspect the target repository before choosing data
models, permission helpers, route names, UI locations, job code, audit-log APIs,
feature-flag names, test commands, or rollout mechanics.

# Nimbus Retention Change Audit Log

## Scope

Create an audit-log entry for every successful retention setting change.

Each entry must record who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
value, and the timestamp.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every successful retention setting change creates exactly the required
  audit-log information: actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.
- Failed or unauthorized attempts do not create successful-change audit entries.
- Audit entries reflect the committed setting change, not an attempted value
  that failed validation or authorization.

## Explicit Exclusions

This ticket does not implement the retention settings UI/API, nightly retention
deletion, rollout enablement, or new legal hold audit behavior.

## References

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md`
- `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`

## Blockers

The target Nimbus application repository is not present in this workspace. An
implementation agent must inspect existing audit-log models, event naming,
actor representation, timestamp conventions, and test helpers before coding.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence showing:

- A successful retention change writes actor, previous value, new value, and
  timestamp.
- Unauthorized attempts do not write a successful-change audit entry.
- Invalid retention values do not write a successful-change audit entry.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

Status: blocked
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Controls Implementation Plan

## Scope

Plan implementation of Nimbus organization-level audit-event retention controls
from the approved Google Doc PRD.

The external Google Doc PRD remains canonical. The active 10x specification
index is `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md`.

This parent ticket is a plan, not an executable implementation ticket.

## Child Tickets

1. `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`
   implements enterprise administrator read/change controls, allowed retention
   values, and the 180-day default.
2. `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-audit-log.md`
   records every successful retention change with actor, previous value, new
   value, and timestamp.
3. `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement.md`
   applies active retention windows in the next nightly retention job without
   inline deletion during settings updates.
4. `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-rollout.md`
   ships the feature behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag using
   the PRD rollout sequence.

## Cross-Ticket Coherence

The settings/access ticket establishes the persisted active retention value used
by audit logging and nightly enforcement. Audit logging must occur only after a
successful change. Nightly enforcement must read the active retention window and
must not depend on inline cleanup during settings updates. Rollout must gate all
user-visible controls and release exposure through the existing enterprise
settings feature flag.

## Explicit Exclusions

This plan does not include legal hold workflows, per-user retention settings,
data export retention, billing plan changes, or retention controls for
non-enterprise workspaces.

## Blockers

Implementation cannot start from this workspace because the Nimbus application
source tree is not present. A future implementation agent must inspect the
target repository and update or execute the child tickets with repository-
specific files, APIs, and verification commands.

## Evidence Expectations

Each child ticket must record evidence for its own acceptance criteria. The
parent can close only after child evidence collectively supports the PRD
acceptance criteria and a review finds no unresolved spec drift.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Created blocked plan from approved PRD export
  `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`, revision
  `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

Status: blocked
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-plan.md
Depends-On: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md

# Nimbus Retention Nightly Enforcement

## Scope

Apply the active organization-level audit-event retention window in the nightly
retention enforcement job.

Shortened retention must take effect on the next nightly job, not inline during
the settings update. Increasing retention preserves events that have not already
expired and does not recreate deleted events.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The nightly retention job uses the workspace active retention window.
- An audit event older than the active retention window and not covered by legal
  hold is eligible for deletion.
- Reducing retention from 180 to 90 days does not delete historical events until
  the next nightly retention job.
- Increasing retention from 90 to 365 days preserves events that still exist.
- Increasing retention does not recreate events already deleted under the
  previous shorter window.
- This implementation does not add legal hold workflows.

## Explicit Exclusions

This ticket does not implement settings read/change authorization, audit-log
entries for setting changes, rollout sequencing, or any legal hold workflow.

## References

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md`
- `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`

## Blockers

The target Nimbus application repository is not present in this workspace. An
implementation agent must inspect the existing nightly retention job, event
storage, deletion semantics, legal-hold representation if any, and test
fixtures before coding.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence showing:

- The nightly job deletes or marks eligible only events older than the active
  retention window.
- Settings updates do not perform inline deletion.
- Existing non-expired events remain after increasing the retention window.
- Deleted events are not recreated after increasing the retention window.
- No legal hold workflow is added by this release.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from approved PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`.

Status: blocked
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-plan.md
Depends-On: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md, .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-audit-log.md, .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-nightly-enforcement.md

# Nimbus Retention Controls Rollout

## Scope

Ship Nimbus retention controls behind the existing enterprise settings feature
flag using the PRD rollout sequence.

The sequence is internal enterprise workspaces first, then ten design partners,
then all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Acceptance Criteria

- User-visible retention controls are gated by the existing enterprise settings
  feature flag.
- Initial enablement is limited to internal enterprise workspaces.
- The next enablement stage is limited to ten design partners.
- Full enterprise enablement occurs only after seven days without severity-one
  incidents.
- Retention controls are not enabled for non-enterprise workspaces in this
  release.

## Explicit Exclusions

This ticket does not implement billing plan changes, non-enterprise retention
controls, or incident-management tooling.

# Nimbus Retention Settings and Access

## Scope

Implement organization-level audit-event retention settings for paid enterprise
workspaces.

This ticket owns the retention value contract, default behavior, and read/change
authorization for the retention control.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise administrators can read the retention setting.
- Enterprise administrators can change the retention setting to exactly one of
  30, 90, 180, or 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces with no explicit retention setting resolve and
  display 180 days.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view the retention
  control.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot read the underlying
  retention value through the implementation surface.
- Non-enterprise workspaces do not receive retention controls in this release.
- Settings updates do not delete historical audit events inline.

## Explicit Exclusions

This ticket does not implement audit-log entry creation, nightly deletion,
legal hold workflows, per-user retention, data export retention, billing plan
changes, or rollout enablement sequencing.

## References

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls.md`
- `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`

## Blockers

The target Nimbus application repository is not present in this workspace. An
implementation agent must inspect the repository before choosing storage,
permission helpers, route/UI surfaces, API contracts, or tests.

## Evidence Expectations

Record evidence showing:

- The default resolves to 180 days for a new enterprise workspace without an
  explicit setting.
- Only 30, 90, 180, and 365 are accepted.
- An enterprise administrator can read and change the setting.
- A non-administrator cannot view the control or read the underlying value.
- A settings update does not trigger inline deletion of audit events.

## Progress and Notes
